title: Terminal int8 HardSwish block
hypothesis: A standalone int8 HardSwish activation block can be physically characterized as a simple bounded circuit_block reference using the existing integer PWL path.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Can one standalone int8 HardSwish block produce accepted physical implementation metrics as another bounded circuit_block reference?
include: one bounded physical sweep on Nangate45
exclude: integrated architecture-block evaluation in the first pass
scaled or fp16 variants in the first pass
follow_on_broad_sweep: only if this standalone reference proves useful for later integrated HardSwish work

expected_benefit: provides real physical timing, power, and area for a bounded HardSwish block
serves as a live end-to-end proof that canonical docs/proposals paths work through queue, review, and finalization
risks: HardSwish semantics are approximated through a quantized PWL fit rather than a built-in primitive
later integrated use may want a different piece set or direct implementation strategy

expected_result.reason: the first goal is simply to obtain accepted physical implementation metrics for one standalone int8 HardSwish block
